humanSummary.text: Delta package audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Remaining prevalidation, artifact-collision, and backslash-path issues are tracked as non-blocking DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Target-manifest completeness is verified after staging writes begin
findings.description: apply_delta validates package internals and entry hashes, then allocates and writes staging files before checking whether the staged tree equals the target manifest.

evidence.detail: Audit found that a partial package can copy/write staging files before the final snapshot check rejects the target mismatch. Final output remains protected.
cargo test -p kaifuu-delta and focused kaifuu-cli delta tests passed during audit.

findings.impact: Output safety is preserved, but fail-before-write behavior is weaker for incomplete multi-entry packages and staging-side effects happen before a deterministic preflight failure.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: apply_delta computes the expected target manifest from source plus changed entries before allocating or writing staging files.
Partial packages that omit required changed entries fail before staging directory allocation.
Regression tests prove no staging writes happen for incomplete multi-entry packages.

proposedDagNode.title: Delta apply preflight completeness
proposedDagNode.summary: Precompute source plus changed-entry target manifests before staging writes so incomplete delta packages fail before any apply-side filesystem mutation.
acceptanceCriteria: Incomplete changed-entry packages fail before staging allocation
Preflight target manifest comparison uses deterministic package data
Regression tests prove no staging files are written for rejected incomplete packages

findings.title: Root patch-result.json can collide with game files
findings.description: The delta snapshot excludes root patch-result.json and the CLI writes its own apply result at that path. A game containing a real root patch-result.json can lose or replace that file while apply reports success.

evidence.detail: Audit observed root patch-result.json reserved in snapshots and reused by CLI output.

findings.impact: A legitimate game file at that reserved path can be silently omitted or overwritten by a command artifact.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Delta apply output artifacts cannot collide with legitimate game files.
If root patch-result.json is reserved, package creation fails when source or target contains that path.
CLI apply can write reports outside the game output tree or to an explicitly requested report path.

proposedDagNode.title: Delta apply report collision safety
proposedDagNode.summary: Prevent .kaifuu apply reports from colliding with real game files by reserving paths explicitly or moving command reports outside patched game output.
deliverables: CLI report-output option or safe default
acceptanceCriteria: A real root patch-result.json in source or target cannot be silently omitted or overwritten
CLI apply writes command reports to a non-colliding location or requires explicit report path selection
Tests cover source collision, target collision, and normal apply report output

findings.title: Backslash path components are ambiguous
findings.description: relative_package_path can serialize a Unix filename containing a backslash, while safe_join_relative later treats backslash as a separator.

evidence.detail: Audit found that a valid Unix name such as a backslash-containing path component can be applied as a slash-separated path or collide with slash paths.

findings.impact: Rare Unix filenames can round-trip to the wrong path or collide with normal slash-separated paths.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Delta package paths reject backslashes consistently or encode path components losslessly.
Tests cover a backslash-containing Unix filename and a slash-path collision case.
Documentation states the v0.2 package path normalization rule.

proposedDagNode.title: Delta package path normalization hardening
proposedDagNode.summary: Harden .kaifuu path normalization so backslash-containing Unix filenames cannot apply as different slash-separated paths or collide with normal paths.
deliverables: Delta package path validation or component encoding
acceptanceCriteria: Backslash-containing filenames are rejected consistently or encoded losslessly
A backslash path cannot apply as a slash-separated path
Regression tests cover backslash and slash-path collision cases
